2026-04-15T23:14:37.9627368Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-15T23:14:37.9628041Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-15T23:14:37.9628877Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-15T23:14:37.9629365Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-15T23:14:37.9629909Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-15T23:14:37.9630128Z [36;1mPORT=3001[0m

2026-04-15T23:14:37.9632175Z [36;1m} >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"[0m

2026-04-15T23:17:14.8072588Z   2. The CHANGELOG shows new features like:
2026-04-15T23:17:14.8073010Z      - Unified Messaging (1.3.0-beta.1)
2026-04-15T23:17:14.8073467Z      - Read receipt and typing indicator (1.3.0-beta.2)
2026-04-15T23:17:14.8074462Z   3. The commit added new classes like `ConversationAdministrationClient`, `ConversationThreadClient`, `ConversationThreadClientSettings`
2026-04-15T23:17:14.8075370Z   
2026-04-15T23:17:14.8076857Z   So the README is missing documentation for the conversation management clients, the Unified Messaging feature overall, and several message types like Interactive, Reaction, and Sticker messages, plus document/video/audio notifications that were introduced in earlier beta versions.
2026-04-15T23:17:14.8078548Z   
2026-04-15T23:17:14.8080066Z   The "Key concepts" section is pretty sparse too—it only covers NotificationMessagesClient. I should examine what the new API surface actually exposes to document it properly.
2026-04-15T23:17:14.8081026Z 
2026-04-15T23:17:14.8081810Z ✓ github-get_file_contents(owner: Azure, path: sdk/communication/Azure.Communication.Me..., ref: main, repo: azure-sdk-for-net)
2026-04-15T23:17:14.8082536Z 
2026-04-15T23:17:14.8083274Z ✓ github-get_file_contents(owner: Azure, path: sdk/communication/Azure.Communication.Me..., ref: main, repo: azure-sdk-for-net)
2026-04-15T23:17:14.8084010Z 
2026-04-15T23:17:14.8084536Z ◆ Now I have a clear picture of the documentation gaps. The README currently only documents:
2026-04-15T23:17:14.8085566Z   1. `NotificationMessagesClient` - for sending notification messages (text, template, media)
2026-04-15T23:17:14.8086302Z   2. `MessageTemplateClient` - for retrieving template information
2026-04-15T23:17:14.8086775Z   
2026-04-15T23:17:14.8087506Z   But the CHANGELOG shows significant new features that are NOT in the README:
2026-04-15T23:17:14.8088315Z   - `ConversationAdministrationClient` (Unified Messaging - added in 1.3.0-beta.1)
2026-04-15T23:17:14.8089122Z   - `ConversationThreadClient` (Unified Messaging - added in 1.3.0-beta.1)
2026-04-15T23:17:14.8090046Z   - Interactive Message, Reaction Message, Sticker Message (1.2.0-beta.1)
2026-04-15T23:17:14.8090608Z   
2026-04-15T23:17:14.8090834Z   
2026-04-15T23:17:14.8091165Z   - Read receipt and typing indicator (1.3.0-beta.2)
2026-04-15T23:17:14.8092042Z   - Document, Video, Audio notification content (1.1.0 - partially covered with media example but using old API)
2026-04-15T23:17:14.8092771Z   
2026-04-15T23:17:14.8095867Z   The "Key concepts" section only mentions `NotificationMessagesClient` and doesn't reflect the full range of clients available. I also notice the "Using statements" section is misplaced—it should be in "Getting started" instead of where it currently sits, and the "Key concepts" header itself seems oddly positioned. Let me examine the ConversationAdministrationClient and ConversationThreadClient APIs to understand what they do.

2026-04-15T23:17:14.8101593Z 
2026-04-15T23:17:14.8102057Z ◆ Now I have enough information to file an issue. The documentation gaps are clear:
2026-04-15T23:17:14.8102650Z   
2026-04-15T23:17:14.8104211Z   1. **README "Key concepts" section is outdated** - It only mentions `NotificationMessagesClient` but does not mention `ConversationAdministrationClient` or `ConversationThreadClient`, which were added as part of the Unified Messaging feature.
2026-04-15T23:17:14.8105723Z   
2026-04-15T23:17:14.8106178Z   2. **Missing Examples for Unified Messaging** - No examples in the README for:
2026-04-15T23:17:14.8106953Z      - `ConversationAdministrationClient` - for managing conversations
2026-04-15T23:17:14.8107747Z      - `ConversationThreadClient` - for sending/receiving conversation messages
2026-04-15T23:17:14.8108364Z   
2026-04-15T23:17:14.8109832Z   3. **Missing documentation for newer message types** - While `ImageNotificationContent` is documented, there's no example for `DocumentNotificationContent`, `VideoNotificationContent`, or `AudioNotificationContent` (added in 1.1.0).
2026-04-15T23:17:14.8111295Z      
2026-04-15T23:17:14.8112338Z   4. **Interactive message types undocumented** - Interactive Message, Reaction Message, and Sticker Message were introduced in 1.2.0-beta.1 but aren't covered in the README.
2026-04-15T23:17:14.8113448Z   
2026-04-15T23:17:14.8114519Z   5. **Structural issues in "Key concepts"** - The authentication and using statements sections are misplaced within "Key concepts" instead of being under "Getting started" where they belong.
2026-04-15T23:17:14.8115678Z   
2026-04-15T23:17:14.8116748Z   The most critical gap is the complete absence of documentation for the new Unified Messaging clients, which represent significant new APIs that should be prominently featured in the README.
2026-04-15T23:17:14.8118065Z   
2026-04-15T23:17:14.8118328Z   I'll create an issue to track this now.

CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 1 issue(s) can be created.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"body":{"description":"Detailed issue description in Markdown. Do NOT repeat the title as a heading since it already appears as the issue's h1. Labels must exist in the repository.","items":{"type":"string"},"type":"array"},"parent":{"description":"Parent issue number for creating sub-issues. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 42 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/42). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123', 'aw_Test123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run.","type":["number","string"]},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"temporary_id":{"description":"Unique temporary identifier for referencing this issue before it's created. Format: 'aw_' followed by 3 to 12 alphanumeric characters (e.g., 'aw_abc1', 'aw_Test123'). Use '#aw_ID' in body text to reference other issues by their temporary_id; these are replaced with actual issue numbers after creation.","pattern":"^aw_[A-Za-z0-9]{3,12}$","type":"string"},"title":{"description":"Concise issue title summarizing the bug, feature, or task. The title appears as the main heading, so keep it brief and descriptive.","type":"string"}},"required":["title","body"],"type":"object"},"name":"create_issue"},{"description":"Report that a tool or capability needed to complete the task is not available, or share any information you deem important about missing functionality or limitations. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because the required functionality is missing or access is restricted.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"alternatives":{"description":"Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"Explanation of why this tool is needed or what information you want to share about the limitation (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"tool":{"description":"Optional: Name or description of the missing tool or capability (max 128 characters). Be specific about what functionality is needed.","type":"string"}},"required":["reason"],"type":"object"},"name":"missing_tool"},{"description":"Log a transparency message when no significant actions are needed. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"message":{"description":"Status or completion message to log. Should explain what was analyzed and the outcome (e.g., 'Code review complete - no issues found', 'Analysis complete - all tests passing').","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":["message"],"type":"object"},"name":"noop"},{"description":"Report that data or information needed to complete the task is not available. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because required data, context, or information is missing.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"alternatives":{"description":"Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"context":{"description":"Additional context about the missing data or where it should come from (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"data_type":{"description":"Type or description of the missing data or information (max 128 characters). Use this when required tools or data are unavailable and the task cannot be meaningfully performed. This is distinct from noop (no action needed) — it indicates an active failure that prevented the task from running. The workflow framework will treat this as a failure signal even when the agent exits successfully.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"details":{"description":"Optional extended details or diagnostic context about the failure (max 65000 characters).","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"A concise explanation of why the task could not be completed (max 1024 characters). Do NOT repeat the title as a heading since it already appears as the issue's h1. Include context, reproduction steps, or acceptance criteria as appropriate.","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"labels":{"description":"Labels to categorize the issue (e.g., 'bug', 'enhancement'). 2026-04-15T23:17:15.0343741Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-15T23:15:54.301535692Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"successfully downloaded text file (SHA: [REDACTED])","type":"text"},{"resource":{"mimeType":"text/plain; charset=utf-8","text":"# Azure Communication Messages client library for .NET\n\nThis package contains a C# SDK for Azure Communication Messages Services.\n\n[Source code][source] | [Package (NuGet)][package] | [Product documentation][product_docs]\n\n\n## Getting started\n\n### Install the package\nInstall the Azure Communication Messages client library for .NET with [NuGet][nuget]:\n\n```dotnetcli\ndotnet add package Azure.Communication.Messages\n``` \n\n### Prerequisites\nYou need an [Azure subscription][azure_sub] and a [Communication Service Resource][communication_resource_docs] to use this package.\n\nTo create a new Communication Service, you can use the [Azure Portal][communication_resource_create_portal], the [Azure PowerShell][communication_resource_create_power_shell], or the [.NET management client library][communication_resource_create_net].\n\nYou must register your WhatsApp business account and have an active channel before sending messages. See [Quickstart: Register WhatsApp business account][azuredocs_register_waba] for more details\n\n### Key concepts\n`NotificationMessagesClient` provides the functionality to send notification messages.\n\n### Using statements\n```C#\nusing Azure.Communication.Messages;\n```\n\n### Authenticate the client\n#### Connection string\n\nThe NotificationMessagesClient is used for sending messages.\nThe MessageTemplateClient is used for retrieving template information.\n\nFor simplicity, this example uses a connection string to authenticate. In production environments, we recommend using Microsoft Entra ID.\n\nMessages clients can be authenticated using the connection string acquired from an Azure Communication Resource in the [Azure Portal][azure_portal].\n\n```C#\nvar connectionString = \"\u003cConnectionString\u003e\"; // Find your Communication Services resource in the Azure portal\nNotificationMessagesClient notificationMessagesClient = new NotificationMessagesClient(connectionString);\nMessageTemplateClient messageTemplateClient = new MessageTemplateClient(connectionString);\n```\n\n## Examples\n### Send a notification message\nTo send a notification message, call the `Send` or `SendAsync` function from the `NotificationMessagesClient`.\n\n#### Send a text message\n```C#\n// Create the recipient list, currently only one recipient is supported \nvar recipientList = new List\u003cstring\u003e { \"\u003cRecipientPhoneNumber\u003e\" };\nvar textContent = new TextNotificationContent(new Guid(\"\u003cchannel-registration-id\u003e\"), recipientList, \"Come on everyone, let's go for lunch together.\");\nSendMessageResult result = await notificationMessagesClient.SendAsync(textContent);\nConsole.WriteLine($\"Message id: {result.Receipts[0].MessageId}\");\n```\n\n#### Send a template message\n```C#\n// Create the recipient list, currently only one recipient is supported \nvar recipientList = new List\u003cstring\u003e { \"\u003cRecipientPhoneNumber\u003e\" };\nstring templateName = \"sample_template\";\nstring templateLanguage = \"en_us\";\nvar messageTemplate = new MessageTemplate(templateName, templateLanguage);\nvar templateContent = new TemplateNotificationContent(channelRegistrationId, recipientList, messageTemplate);\nSendMessageResult result = await notificationMessagesClient.SendAsync(templateContent);\nConsole.WriteLine($\"Message id: {result.Receipts[0].MessageId}\");\n```\n\n#### Send a media message\n```C#\n// Create the recipient list, currently only one recipient is supported \nvar recipientList = new List\u003cstring\u003e { \"\u003cRecipientPhoneNumber\u003e\" };\nvar uri = new Uri(\"https://aka.ms/acsicon1\");\nvar mediaContent = new ImageNotificationContent(channelRegistrationId, recipientList, uri);\nSendMessageResult result = await notificationMessagesClient.SendAsync(mediaContent);\nConsole.WriteLine($\"Message id: {result.Receipts[0].MessageId}\");\n```\n\n### Retrieve templates\nTo retrieve templates, call the `GetTemplates` or `GetTemplatesAsync` function from the `MessageTemplateClient`.\n\n```C#\nAsyncPageable\u003cMessageTemplateItem\u003e templates = messageTemplateClient.GetTemplatesAsync(channelId);\nawait foreach (MessageTemplateItem template in templates)\n{\n    Console.WriteLine($\"{template.Name}\");\n}\n```\n\n## Troubleshooting\nA `RequestFailedException` is thrown as a service response for any unsuccessful requests. The exception contains information about what response code was returned from the service.\n\n## Next steps\n- Read more about Messages in Azure Communication Services at [Advanced Messaging for WhatsApp in Azure Communication Services][azuredocs_whatsapp_overview].\n- Read more about how to set up an Event Grid subscription to receive messages and message delivery statuses at [Quickstart: Handle Advanced Messaging events][azuredocs_eventgrid_setup].\n- Read more about how to use templates at [Send WhatsApp template messages][azuredocs_templates].\n- For a set-by-step guide on how to use the Messages APIs, see [Quickstart: Send WhatsApp Messages using Advanced Messages][azuredocs_quickstart]].\n\n\n## Contributing\nThis project welcomes contributions and suggestions. Most contributions require you to agree to a Contributor License Agreement (CLA) declaring that you have the right to, and actually do, grant us the rights to use your contribution. For details, visit [cla.microsoft.com][cla].\n\nThis project has adopted the [Microsoft Open Source Code of Conduct][coc]. For more information see the [Code of Conduct FAQ][coc_faq] or contact [opencode@microsoft.com][coc_contact] with any additional questions or comments.\n\n\u003c!-- LINKS --\u003e\n[source]: A deprecation warning will inform users to transition to the new `ImageNotificationContent` model for future use.\n\n## 1.0.0 (2024-02-29)\n\n### Features Added\n- Introduced additional constructors for `MessageTemplateClient` and `NotificationMessagesClient` to support `TokenCredential` authentication methods.\n- Introduced a variety of Notification Content models such as `MediaNotificationContent`, `TemplateNotificationContent`, and `TextNotificationContent`, enabling more polymorphic notification types.\n- Introduced a variety of message template value models such as `MessageTemplateDocument`, `MessageTemplateImage`, `MessageTemplateVideo`, etc., enabling more polymorphic message template values.\n- Added new namespace specific to WhatsApp message templates, including `WhatsAppMessageTemplateBindings`, `WhatsAppMessageTemplateBindingsButton`, and `WhatsAppMessageButtonSubType`, etc.\n- Updated `CommunicationMessagesClientOptions` to include a new service version `V2024_02_01` and e
